The Chain Museum Co., Ltd. announced that it has received funding from Resona Capital Co., Ltd., IGNITIONPOINT Venture Partners Inc., Goldwin Venture Partners Co., Ltd., Daibiru Corporation, NANKAI NEXT Ventures Co., Ltd.
data.description:
The Chain Museum Co., Ltd. announced that it has received round of funding on October 16, 2025. The transaction included the participation from new investors Resona Capital Investment Limited Partnership No. 8, a fund managed by Resona Capital Co., Ltd., Goldwin Play Earth Fund, a fund managed by Goldwin Venture Partners Co., Ltd, IGNITIONPOINT Venture Partners Inc.., Daibiru Corporation, NANKAI NEXT Ventures Co., Ltd. The company issued the funds through a third-party allotment of new shares method.

The company has issued convertible preferred stock in the transaction.

U.S. Bancorp Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
U.S. Bancorp reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported net interest income was USD 4,222 million compared to USD 4,135 million a year ago. Net income was USD 2,001 million compared to USD 1,714 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 1.22 compared to USD 1.03 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 1.22 compared to USD 1.03 a year ago.
For the nine months, net interest income was USD 12,365 million compared to USD 12,143 million a year ago. Net income was USD 5,525 million compared to USD 4,636 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 3.36 compared to USD 2.77 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 3.35 compared to USD 2.77 a year ago.

Zhejiang Cfmoto Power Co.,Ltd Reports Earnings Results for the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Zhejiang Cfmoto Power Co.,Ltd reported earnings results for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the nine months, the company reported sales was CNY 14,896.33 million compared to CNY 11,450.03 million a year ago. Revenue was CNY 14,896.33 million compared to CNY 11,450.03 million a year ago.

Net income was CNY 1,415.37 million compared to CNY 1,081.31 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 9.29 compared to CNY 7.16 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 9.17 compared to CNY 7.11 a year ago.

data.title: The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported net income was USD 1,445 million compared to USD 1,182 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 1.9 compared to USD 1.51 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 1.88 compared to USD 1.5 a year ago.
For the nine months, net income was USD 4,088 million compared to USD 3,375 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 5.43 compared to USD 4.29 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 5.39 compared to USD 4.26 a year ago.

data.title: Snap-on Incorporated Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 27, 2025
data.description:
Snap-on Incorporated reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 27, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was USD 1,190.8 million compared to USD 1,147 million a year ago. Net income was USD 265.4 million compared to USD 251.1 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 5.09 compared to USD 4.77 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 5.02 compared to USD 4.7 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was USD 3,511.3 million compared to USD 3,508.7 million a year ago. Net income was USD 756.2 million compared to USD 785.8 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 14.49 compared to USD 14.91 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 14.24 compared to USD 14.69 a year ago.

data.title: Marsh & McLennan Companies, Inc. Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Marsh & McLennan Companies, Inc. reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was USD 6,351 million compared to USD 5,697 million a year ago. Net income was USD 747 million compared to USD 747 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 1.52 compared to USD 1.52 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 1.51 compared to USD 1.51 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was USD 20,386 million compared to USD 18,391 million a year ago. Net income was USD 3,339 million compared to USD 3,272 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 6.79 compared to USD 6.65 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 6.75 compared to USD 6.59 a year ago.

data.title: SIMO Appoints Td Synnex as Distribution Partner
data.description:
SIMO announced the appointment of TD SYNNEX as its distribution partner inside the SIMO Alliance Program. Through this exciting collaboration, SIMO will leverage TD SYNNEX's unmatched global scale, logistics, and channel expertise to bring its advanced multi-carrier connectivity solutions to more partners and customers around the world. By joining forces, SIMO and TD SYNNEX will provide VARs, MSPs, and other solution providers with industry-leading, AI-enhanced cellular connectivity solutions and easy access through TD SYNNEX's global distribution network.

SIMO's vSIM technology eliminates the need for physical SIM cards by enabling devices to dynamically connect to multiple carrier networks worldwide, ensuring seamless connectivity across borders. Starting now, SIMO and SIMO- Inside products, including Solis mobile hotspots and CPE routers, are available directly through the TD SYNNEX portal with additional products on the horizon. Businesses and resellers are invited to join the SIMO Alliance Partner Program to access exclusive benefits, commissions, and co-marketing support.

VICI Properties Inc. Enters into Agreements Relating to MGM Northfield Park in Northfield, OH
data.description:
VICI Properties Inc. announced that, in connection with MGM Resorts International's agreement to sell the operations of MGM Northfield Park ("Northfield Park"), located in Northfield, OH, to an affiliate of funds managed by Clairvest Group Inc. VICI has agreed to enter into a new separate lease with an affiliate of Clairvest with respect to the real property of Northfield Park (the "Northfield Park Lease") and also enter into an amendment to the Master Lease between VICI Properties and MGM Resorts (the "MGM Master Lease"). The Northfield Park Lease will have an initial annual base rent of $53.0 million, and if closing occurs after May 1, 2026, such base rent shall be $54.0 million, reflecting the 2.0% annual escalation provided for in the MGM Master Lease. Upon closing, the Northfield Park Lease will begin a new 25-year lease term with three 10-year renewal options.

The other terms of the Northfield Park Lease will be substantially the same as the MGM Master Lease, including escalation of 2.0% per annum (with escalation equal to the greater of 2.0% and the change in CPI (capped at 3.0%) beginning at the same time as the MGM Master Lease in 2032) and a minimum capital expenditure requirement equal to 1.0% of annual net revenue. The Northfield Park Lease will be guaranteed by an affiliate of funds managed by Clairvest that will own the operations of Northfield Park. VICI Properties has also agreed, in accordance with, and as contemplated by, the terms of the MGM Master Lease, to amend the MGM Master Lease to account for MGM?s divestiture of the operations of Northfield Park, which will result in a reduction of the annual base rent under the MGM Master Lease by $53.0 million (or $54.0 million should the closing occur after May 1, 2026).

data.title: Bank of Montreal Announces Launch of Five New Canadian Depositary Receives Five New Canadian Depositary Receives Offering Investors Exposure to U.S. Companies
data.description:
Bank of Montreal (BMO) announced the launch of five new Canadian deposit receipts (CDRs) offering investors exposure to U.S. companies. The initial offering of the new CDRs has closed and the five new CDRs will begin trading on the Cboe Canada exchange today. BMO's new CDRs will trade under the following tickers: Company Name and Type of Shares CDR Ticker Jurisdiction: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. ZAMD U.S. Common Shares; Al Alphabet Inc. ZGOO U.S. Class C Capital Shares; Amazon.com Inc. ZAMZ U.S. Common Shares.

NVIDIA Corporation ZNVD U.S. Common Shares and Tesla Inc. ZTSL U.S. Common Shares, Manage Currency Risk: BMO's CDRs minimize the risk associated with currency fluctuations when trading international companies, eliminating the need to convert Canadian dollars to foreign currency. Managing currency risk allows investors to focus on a company's fundamentals when making investment decisions. CDRs are a simple and effective way to gain exposure to some of the largest companies with a global reach.

Buying a single stock in foreign markets like the U.S., Europe and Asia may be a difficult task for investors based in Canada, but BMO's CDRs trade on Cboe Canada, offering easy access to these companies across several platforms for individual investors or through a financial advisor.
